# Holotope.js

> TypeScript toolkit for accountable higher-dimensional geometry: authoritative
> R^N state, explicit projection or slicing, traceable source identity, and
> source-space simulation. Experimental API; target ES modules.

## Read this first

- README.md — installation, package overview, runnable public examples.
- docs/mental-model.md — package responsibilities and the source → representation → interaction loop.
- docs/cookbook.md — verified task-oriented snippets.
- docs/representation-provenance.md — what a pick can and cannot recover.
- docs/physics.md — rigid mechanics, contact capability boundaries, and RN XPBD.

## Package map

- `@holotope/core`: zero-dependency Float64 N-D math, geometry, polytopes,
  projections, slicing, source references, fields, lattices, spectral analysis.
- `@holotope/physics`: headless R4 rigid bodies and RN point/XPBD systems.
  It never renders and it does not automatically make all registered rigid
  bodies collide.
- `@holotope/three`: Three.js peer-dependency adapters. Products expose an
  ordinary `.object` and must be updated from authoritative source state.
- `@holotope/three/webgpu`: optional WebGPU/TSL realization paths. Preserve
  the Float64 CPU path for validation and source-space semantics.

## Non-negotiable model rules

1. Keep the source in R^N. Do not simulate or edit the projected Three mesh as
   though it were the source object.
2. Projection can be ambiguous; an exact slice is a different observation.
3. A `RepresentationHitN` always identifies a source record when supported;
   use `ambientPoint` only when `ambientPointStatus === 'exact'`.
4. `RigidBody4` / `PhysicsWorld4` and `XpbdParticleN` / `XpbdWorldN` are
   separate simulation layers.
5. All R4 vectors use `[x, y, z, w]`. R4 bivectors use
   `[xy, xz, xw, yz, yw, zw]` = `[01, 02, 03, 12, 13, 23]`.

## Primary imports

```ts
// @holotope/core
import {
  VecN, MatN, BivectorN, Rotor4, TransformN,
  CellComplex, createHypercube, tetrahedralizeCuboidCells,
  PerspectiveProjection, CoordinateProjection, HyperplaneSlice4
} from '@holotope/core';

// @holotope/physics
import {
  massPropertiesFromCellComplex4, RigidBody4, PhysicsWorld4,
  XpbdParticleN, XpbdWorldN, compileXpbdParticleBindingN,
  compileXpbdParticleHyperplaneFamilyN, predictXpbdInertialStateN,
  evaluateXpbdPotentialStateN, evaluateXpbdIncrementalPotentialN,
  compileXpbdIncrementalPotentialProblemN,
  searchXpbdIncrementalPotentialArmijoN,
  minimizeXpbdIncrementalPotentialN,
  applyXpbdIncrementalPotentialResultN
} from '@holotope/physics';

// @holotope/three
import {
  ProjectedEdges3D, ProjectedSurface3D, SlicedComplex3D, DragRotation4D,
  representationHitFromProjectedEdge,
  representationHitFromProjectedSurface,
  representationHitFromSlicedComplex
} from '@holotope/three';
```

## Core anchors

- Vectors/transforms: `VecN`, `MatN`, `BivectorN`, `Rotor4`, `TransformN`.
- Topology: `CellComplex`, cuboid tetrahedralization, source-cell references.
- Observation: `PerspectiveProjection`, `CoordinateProjection`,
  `OrthographicProjection`, `HyperplaneSlice4`, inverse fibres and map lineage.
- Fields: quaternion and bicomplex Julia evaluators, generic samples and
  isosurface records.
- Scientific structures: regular polychora, Coxeter/Wythoff construction,
  model sets, graph-Laplacian modes, couplings.

Canonical complete core export barrel: `packages/core/src/index.ts`.

## Physics anchors

- Rigid: `massPropertiesFromCellComplex4`, `RigidBody4`, `PhysicsWorld4`,
  `RigidBodyObject4Binding`.
- Rigid collision/query: `HyperplaneColliderN`, `Hyperbox4`, support shapes,
  GJK, EPA, linear casts, narrowphase dispatch, named contact pipelines.
- Rigid constraints: point, distance, direction, orientation, planar rotation,
  motors, and bounded blocks.
- RN point/XPBD: `XpbdConstraintSolverN`, `XpbdParticleN`, `XpbdWorldN`,
  particle binding, distance-network, simplex-measure, StVK family, and
  particle--hyperplane contact families.
- Simplex constitutive references: `SimplexConstitutiveEvaluationN`,
  `evaluateSimplexStVenantKirchhoffN`, and
  `evaluateSimplexCompressibleNeoHookeanN`. The optional
  `evaluateSimplexMeasureBarrierN` / `simplexMeasureBarrierLawN` pair supplies
  a C2-clamped lower-measure energy and analytic gradient.
- Constitutive assembly: `SimplexConstitutiveLawN`, built-in immutable law
  descriptors, `compileSimplexConstitutiveFamilyN`, plus named StVK and
  Neo-Hookean family compilers.
- Candidate objectives: `XpbdConservativeForceProviderN`, family
  `evaluateAt(positionOf)`, and `evaluateXpbdPotentialStateN` evaluate and
  assemble trial-state energy plus `dU/dx` without mutating live particles.
- Incremental objectives: `predictXpbdInertialStateN` constructs non-mutating
  inertial centers; `evaluateXpbdIncrementalPotentialN` returns
  `1/2 sum m||q-qHat||^2 + h^2 U(q)` and its free-coordinate gradient.
- Solver view: `compileXpbdIncrementalPotentialProblemN` packs dynamic
  particle axes; `searchXpbdIncrementalPotentialArmijoN` performs auditable
  sufficient-decrease backtracking. Only typed
  `SimplexConstitutiveDomainErrorN` trials are recoverable.
- Reference minimization: `minimizeXpbdIncrementalPotentialN` repeatedly uses
  the negative packed gradient and Armijo search under explicit iteration,
  trial, and gradient-tolerance bounds. It returns converged, iteration-limit,
  line-search-exhausted, or stalled evidence and retains its exact problem.
- Atomic application: `applyXpbdIncrementalPotentialResultN` applies only a
  converged result after proving live-state and result-evidence freshness.
  Default dynamic velocity is `(qNext - qBefore) / h`; fixed velocity is
  preserved and external force clears. Refusals do not write partial state.
- A measure barrier can be compiled as a second generic constitutive family on
  the same simplex group and exact particles. It is a proactive force, not an
  inversion guarantee; retain endpoint and signed/intrinsic chord guards.
- Accepted RN state: `XpbdStateGuardN`,
  `compileSimplexConstitutiveFamilyStateGuardN`,
  `analyzeLinearSimplexOrientationN`,
  `compileSimplexConstitutiveFamilyTrajectoryGuardN`,
  `analyzeLinearSimplexMeasureN`,
  `compileSimplexConstitutiveFamilyMeasureTrajectoryGuardN`, and bounded
  transactional `XpbdWorldN.stepAdaptive()`. Use the signed pair for an
  N-simplex in R^N and the intrinsic Gram-determinant pair for a k-simplex in
  R^N. Both certify only a linear substep chord and are not nonlinear barriers
  or formal interval-arithmetic proofs.
- RN velocity policies: `XpbdVelocityResponseN`,
  `compileXpbdParticleHyperplaneFrictionFamilyN` (complete Coulomb tangent
  ball), and `XpbdExponentialVelocityDampingN` (inverse-seconds decay).

Canonical complete physics export barrel: `packages/physics/src/index.ts`.
Candidate-state objective guide: `docs/candidate-potentials.md`.
Incremental objective guide: `docs/incremental-potentials.md`.

## Three anchors

- `ProjectedEdges3D`: projected 1-skeleton render product.
- `ProjectedSurface3D`: projected 2-face mesh with source-triangle picking.
- `SlicedComplex3D`: exact 4D section from tetrahedral 3-cells.
- `SampledSlicedField3D`, `FieldRelief3D`: field visualization products.
- `DragRotation4D`: pointer deltas → visual `Rotor4`, default Alt drag in xw/yw.
- `representationHitFrom…`: map a Three ray intersection to source-aware
  `RepresentationHitN`.

Canonical complete Three export barrel: `packages/three/src/index.ts`.

## Minimal, accurate patterns

```ts
// Project and slice the same R4 geometry.
const complex = tetrahedralizeCuboidCells(createHypercube({ dim: 4, size: 2 }));
const pose = TransformN.identity(4);
const shadow = new ProjectedEdges3D(
  complex, new PerspectiveProjection({ fromDim: 4, viewDistance: 4 })
);
const section = new SlicedComplex3D(complex, HyperplaneSlice4.axisAligned(3, 0));
scene.add(shadow.object, section.object);
shadow.update(pose);
section.update(pose);
```

```ts
// Sweep a W slice; this is an exact cross-section, not a camera motion.
slice.offset = 0.8 * Math.sin(time);
slice.setNormal([0.15, 0, 0, 1]); // normalizes and keeps a continuous frame
section.update(pose);
```

```ts
// R4 physical angular velocity: coefficients are xy, xz, xw, yz, yw, zw.
body.setAngularVelocityWorld(BivectorN.fromPlanes(4, [
  { i: 0, j: 3, angle: 0.6 },
  { i: 1, j: 2, angle: -0.2 }
]));
world.step(1 / 120);
surface.update(new TransformN(4, body.rotation, body.position));
```

```ts
// Small-system Float64 golden path; this does not apply the result.
const result = minimizeXpbdIncrementalPotentialN({
  problem,
  initialCoordinates: problem.packPositions(candidatePositions),
  gradientTolerance: 1e-8,
  maximumIterations: 128
});
if (result.status === 'converged') {
  inspectCandidate(result.final.positions);
}
```

```ts
// Explicit state transition; expected staleness is returned, not hidden.
const application = applyXpbdIncrementalPotentialResultN({
  result,
  velocityUpdate: 'backward-euler',
  clearForces: true
});
if (application.status === 'refused') {
  inspectRefusal(application);
}
```

```ts
// Pick a projected triangle. Exact ambient lift depends on projection and hit.
const hit = representationHitFromProjectedSurface(surface, intersection);
if (hit.ambientPointStatus === 'exact') {
  useSourcePoint(hit.ambientPoint);
}
```

## Avoid these invented APIs or assumptions

- Do not expect a `PhysicsWorld4.addBody()` call alone to create collisions.
- Do not expect projected picks to always yield a unique R4 point.
- Do not create a `SlicedComplex3D` from a raw cuboid complex without the
  required tetrahedral 3-cells.
- Do not treat `DragRotation4D` as physical angular velocity or torque.
- Do not guess exports: inspect the canonical barrel listed above when the
  needed feature is not in this file.
